manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: spot, eagle, cover, drill, mail, ice, box, brush, mine.
Your opponent's team words are: witch, france, ghost, point, unicorn, chair, rabbit, comic.
Distractor words are: horseshoe, saturn, glove.
The kill words are: concert, state, ivory, chest, lemon.

message.reasoning:

Need to choose which of our words to target, maybe multiple. Must avoid the clue unintentionally connecting to opponent/distractor/kill words? The clue can relate to opponent's words but that would risk partner guessing them incorrectly. We should choose a clue that strongly relates to our words and not to others. Let's examine our words: spot, eagle, cover, drill (as noun?), mail, ice, box, brush, mine.

We need a clue that can associate with many of them. Perhaps "metal"? Could relate to "mail" (mail armor), "box" (metal box), "cover" (metal cover), "drill" (metal drill), "eagle" (metal eagle? maybe metal eagle statue?), "spot" (metal spot). Might be too broad. Also "mail" could be chainmail (metal). However "ice" & "brush" less; "mine" (as noun explosive) metal? Could be "steel". That might link to drill, box, cover, mail, mine (steel mine?), eagle (steel eagle?), spot (steel spot? not strong). Might cause opponent words: "unicorn" not metal; "ghost" maybe not; "witch" not; "point" maybe metal point; "chair" could be metal; "horse shoe" is metal, which is a distractor; that could be risky: clue "steel" might lead to horse shoe (distractor). Avoid.

Maybe "north"? Spot? Eagle? Not. "Cold"? Ice, mail? Not.

Maybe "delivery"? Mail, box, drill? Wait drill as "drill" for training? Not.

Maybe "forest"? Eagle, brush, spot? Not.

We could aim for a clue that matches three or four words; maybe "construction": drill, box, cover, mine? Not.

"Mining"? Mine, drill, box (mining box?), mail? "mail" as "post"? Not.

"Paper"? Spot (spot of ink), mail, box (mailbox), cover (cover of a book). That's good: clue "paper". That could relate to mail (paper letter), box (paper box), cover (cover of a book made of paper), spot (a spot of ink on paper). Also maybe brush (paintbrush on paper), ice (paper ice? no). Eagle (paper eagle? maybe origami?), drill (paper drill? no). Avoid opponent words: "ghost", "witch" not; "point" maybe paper point? Not. "unicorn" maybe paper unicorn figures; but less likely. Distractor "horseshoe" no.

Kill words: "concert", "state", "ivory", "chest", "lemon" - paper is not.

Paper seems safe but must ensure not part of any listed words. It's not. Provide list of targets: mail, box, cover, spot (maybe). Might be ambiguous if partner might guess "brush"? Paper brush? Not strong. Better include only those strongly linked: mail, box, cover, spot maybe.

But "cover" is paper cover (book cover), "box" paper box, "mail" paper letter. "spot" may be less obvious but a "spot of ink" on paper. Could be okay.

Alternatively "post"? Relates to mail, spot (post spot? no), cover (post cover? maybe not). Not good.

"Envelope"? But that is a word that is not allowed? It's not on list, but it's too specific to mail/box. We can only give a clue word that's one word. "Envelope" relates to mail and box maybe, but not cover.

Maybe "ink": relates to spot (ink spot), brush (ink brush), mail (inked letters), box (ink box? no). Could be risky.

Better to propose "paper". Provide target list: mail, box, cover, spot.

Check opponent words: "ghost": could be paper ghost? maybe not. "unicorn": paper unicorn could be origami but less likely. "chair": paper chair maybe but not common. "witch", "france", "comic": not.

Distractor "horseshoe": paper horseshoe? less likely. "saturn", "glove": no.

Kill words: "concert": paper concert? no. "state": paper state? not. "ivory": paper ivory? no. "chest": paper chest? no. "lemon": paper lemon? not.

Thus safe.

Return in format.
